first: New flat. New city. The canal is outside.

neutral: Basin still. Boats still. I'm still.
Narrow Street is narrow. The name is honest.
A heron on the lock gate. Absolutely still.
The church is too big for this street.
Iron and water. Limehouse's two materials.
Every building here has outlived its purpose.
Someone was here before. Someone will be after.
The air tastes different near the water.
A window lit. A life in progress.
Limehouse holds its breath at corners.
The walls know more than the maps.

time.morning: Coffee steam from the shop. A signal.
time.afternoon: The afternoon light makes Limehouse look gentle.
time.evening: The canal at dusk. Everything gold and grey.
time.night: Limehouse at night is a different proposition.

rain: Rain on the canal. A million tiny impacts.
The basin vanishes in the downpour. Only sound confirms it's there.

musician: A hum under the bridge. Low. Constant.
The chain clinks in rhythm. Almost musical.
Something in the water. A frequency. Low.
The whole neighbourhood vibrates. One note.
Every surface here is an instrument.
The city plays itself. Nobody conducts.
Footsteps on stone. A rhythm you didn't choose.
The city hums between its words.
Every door is a different key.
Silence here isn't empty. It's tuned.

leaving: Door. Street. London. Here we go.
The notebook has more pages filled now.
Something to do today. Someone to see.
Lock the door. The city is waiting.
One more walk. One more look.
returning: Home. Or what I'm calling home.
The flat looks different every time.
The radiator. The window. Yours now.
Back. The notebook is where you left it.
